skill.name: routeros-app-yaml
skill.description: RouterOS /app YAML format for container applications (7.21+ builtin app, 7.22+ custom YAML creation). Use when: writing or validating RouterOS /app YAML files, working with MikroTik container apps, building docker-compose-like definitions for RouterOS, creating /app store schemas, debugging /app validation errors, or when the user mentions /app, tikapp, or RouterOS container YAML.
skill.summary: Generate and validate RouterOS /app YAML definitions for container applications
skill.author: tikoci

security_audit.summary: This skill is a pure documentation resource containing markdown files explaining RouterOS /app YAML format. The static scanner flagged 159 potential issues, but all are false positives: backtick characters in markdown syntax were misidentified as Ruby/shell execution; YAML example keys were misidentified as cryptographic or reconnaissance patterns; and placeholder URLs were flagged as hardcoded network calls. After manual review, no actual security risks exist in this documentation-only skill.

content.user_title: Generate RouterOS container app YAML configurations
content.value_statement: Writing RouterOS /app YAML files by hand is error-prone due to unique syntax differences from docker-compose. This skill helps you generate valid YAML for MikroTik container applications with proper port formats, environment placeholders, and store schema.
actual_capabilities: Generate valid RouterOS /app YAML definitions with proper syntax for services, volumes, networks, and configs
Explain differences between docker-compose and RouterOS /app YAML format
Validate /app YAML against JSON schema variants (latest vs editor)
Demonstrate port mapping formats (old OCI-style and new RouterOS 7.23+ colon style)
Create app store schema files (.tikappstore.yaml) for curated app collections
Show placeholder usage ([accessIP], [containerIP], [routerIP]) for dynamic configuration
limitations: Does not execute RouterOS commands or interact with live devices
Does not provide container runtime or deployment functionality
Schema validation examples are illustrative; actual validation requires VSCode extension or CI tooling

use_cases.title: Network engineer managing MikroTik routers
use_cases.description: Deploy container applications on RouterOS 7.22+ routers by generating valid /app YAML definitions for apps like nginx, PostgreSQL, or Grafana.

use_cases.title: DevOps engineer building MikroTik app stores
use_cases.description: Create curated app collections by writing .tikappstore.yaml files that group multiple /app definitions for easy distribution.

use_cases.title: Developer migrating from docker-compose
use_cases.description: Convert existing docker-compose.yml files to RouterOS /app YAML format, understanding key syntax differences and limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a basic app
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a RouterOS /app YAML for a [container-name] container using the [image] image, exposing port [port].

prompt_templates.title: Generate a multi-service app
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a RouterOS /app YAML with a web service using [web-image] and a database using [db-image]. The web service depends on the database and uses port [port].

prompt_templates.title: Create an app store schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a RouterOS app store YAML file with three apps: [app1-name] using [app1-image], [app2-name] using [app2-image], and [app3-name] using [app3-image].

prompt_templates.title: Debug YAML validation errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this RouterOS /app YAML and explain why validation fails: [paste YAML here]. Focus on port format, environment variable naming, and supported properties.

output_examples.input: Create a RouterOS /app YAML for nginx with port 8080 mapped to container port 80
output_examples.output: Generates YAML with name, services.web.image (nginx:alpine), and ports configuration using the correct RouterOS syntax.

output_examples.input: Explain the difference between old OCI-style and new RouterOS port formats
output_examples.output: Shows side-by-side comparison: '8080:80/tcp:web' (old) vs '8080:80:web:tcp' (new 7.23+ style) with protocol placement differences.

output_examples.input: Create a multi-service app with nginx frontend and PostgreSQL backend
output_examples.output: Generates full YAML with web and db services, volumes for persistent storage, network configuration, and environment variable placeholders.

best_practices: Use the .tikapp.yaml file extension for app definitions and .tikappstore.yaml for app store collections
Use uppercase with underscores for environment variable names (POSTGRES_DB) to pass strict schema validation
Reference the skill documentation to understand key docker-compose differences like missing deploy/resources support
anti_patterns: Do not use docker-compose version key; RouterOS ignores it
Do not mix OCI-style and new colon-style port formats in the same port string
Do not assume bind mount options are supported; RouterOS /app only supports named volumes

faq.question: What RouterOS version is required for /app YAML support?
faq.answer: /app builtin is available in RouterOS 7.21+. Full custom YAML app creation (/app/add) requires 7.22 or later.

faq.question: How is RouterOS /app different from docker-compose?
faq.answer: Key differences include port format syntax, simplified networks, no bind mount options, no deploy/resources, and placeholder expansion for IPs and ports.

faq.question: What container images work with RouterOS /app?
faq.answer: Standard OCI container images from any registry work. Configure the default registry URL in /container/config or specify full image paths.

faq.question: How do placeholders work in /app YAML?
faq.answer: RouterOS expands [accessIP], [accessPort], [containerIP], and [routerIP] at deploy time for dynamic configuration of services.

faq.question: Can I use VSCode for /app YAML validation?
faq.answer: Yes. Add the schema URLs to yaml.schemas in VSCode settings. Use .editor.json URLs for better autocompletion.

faq.question: What extra package is needed for /app to work?
faq.answer: The container extra package must be installed. Without it, the /app endpoint returns 404.
